headline: World Cup 2026 footballers compete with potentially dangerous temperatures
description: Heat is expected to disrupt play during the tournament, which is directly affected by climate change. According to a study by the World Weather Attribution scientific network, 26 matches are expected to take place in conditions that pose health risks for athletes.

headline: The endless 'wandering' of exiles at Paris's Stalingrad metro station

France bids farewell to Lyhanna, 11, whose killing sparked outrage
description: The body of the young girl was found last week after she went missing on May 29 in the southwestern town of Fleurance.

headline: Boycott of Israeli filmmaker Nadav Lapid: 'A creator should not be reduced to their passport. It is time to stop denying their words, their actions and their work'
description: The support extended to the Israeli director by numerous French film figures contrasts with the failure of cultural institutions and political leaders to do the same – even when the targeted artists are outspoken opponents of their own country's policies, writes Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin.

headline: Study reveals first global map of underground fungal networks vital for plant life
description: The journal 'Science' published on Thursday the world's first planetary mapping of the density of mycorrhizal fungal filaments that colonize the roots of 70% of land plant species, forming vital symbiotic relationships. The work is also a call to protect the health of these fungal networks.

headline: David Hockney, giant of British painting, dies at the age of 88
description: Internationally renowned, notably for his painting 'A Bigger Splash' (1967) and his series of pool paintings, the British artist, who lived in Normandy from 2019 to 2023, produced work that straddled pop art and hyperrealism, never hesitating to use new technologies as sources of inspiration. He died on June 11.

The murky ties between FIFA and the US
description: After the 2022 World Cup was awarded to Qatar, the US launched a legal offensive against several FIFA members before securing the co-hosting of the 2026 tournament. This is the story of the rise of American soft power in global football.

headline: Bardella's contradiction of Le Pen on pensions confounds French far right
description: Jordan Bardella, who would stand as the Rassemblement National's presidential candidate if Marine Le Pen's election ban is upheld, has broken with his boss's line on a crucial policy issue.

headline: 'Lyhanna was a victim twice': Women-led rallies across France condemn failures after schoolgirl's death
description: Several thousand people gathered on Monday in front of courthouses and city halls to express their dismay at the judicial treatment of sexual violence committed against children. Many demonstrators called for the resignation of Justice Minister Gérald Darmanin.
